name: Free Tax Prep Los Angeles
description: LA City public-private collaboration through CIFD providing free VITA tax preparation and ensuring eligible Angelenos claim federal EITC and CalEITC
benefit_formula: Average combined refund unlocked plus avoided tax-prep fees for qualifying filer

name: FamilySource System (FSS)
description: Nineteen LA City centers in high-need areas providing case management ESL tax prep tutoring and navigation services to low-income Angelenos
benefit_formula: Estimated annual value of case management and navigation services

name: Rent Stabilization Ordinance Reform
description: LA City rent control caps annual rent increases at 1 to 4 percent for tenants in covered units (most apartments built before October 1978)
benefit_formula: Estimated annual savings versus uncapped market increase for renter in covered unit

name: We Are LA
description: LA Mayor's Fund tenant protection initiative providing free legal hotline rights education and outreach to renters
benefit_formula: Estimated annual value of legal navigation and tenant rights services

name: ULA Income Support Program
description: Measure ULA-funded $1000 per month income support for severely rent-burdened seniors and persons with disabilities (limited enrollment)

name: LADWP EZ-SAVE
description: LADWP utility discount for income-qualified residential customers (flat subsidy on electric and water plus 31 percent off sewer charge)
benefit_formula: $16.34 electric plus $10 water bi-monthly subsidy plus sewer discount

name: LADWP Senior and Disability Lifeline Rate
description: LADWP utility rate reduction for residents who are 62 or older or have a disability and meet income limits (combined household AGI under $47300)

name: Sanitation Lifeline Reimbursement
description: LA City Bureau of Sanitation solid waste fee reimbursement for low-income seniors and persons with disabilities
benefit_formula: Annual reimbursement of solid resources fee

name: Cityride
description: LADOT subsidized transportation program providing $84 in fares per quarter to seniors 65+ and qualified persons with disabilities in LA City

name: Summer Lunch Program
description: LA Recreation and Parks free nutritionally-balanced lunches for children ages 1 to 18 at 100 LA recreation centers during summer
benefit_formula: 60 weekdays x $4 per meal per child

name: Summer Youth Employment Program
description: LA City Economic and Workforce Development Department paid summer employment for youth ages 14 to 24
benefit_formula: 140 hours at LA City minimum wage

name: Youth Service Corps
description: LA Mayor's Office and EWDD initiative providing paid public service work and career pathways for LA youth
benefit_formula: Estimated stipend for participating youth (varies by placement)

name: PlayLA Youth and Adaptive Sports
description: LA Recreation and Parks free youth sports programming including adaptive sports for children ages 3 to 17 across the city
benefit_formula: Estimated annual value of avoided sports and activity fees

name: House Our Vets
description: Mayor Bass operational campaign to place homeless veterans in permanent housing by expanding landlord participation in HUD-VASH vouchers (administered by HACLA + LAHSA + VA)
benefit_formula: Annual rent value at LA median 1BR rent

name: Continuum of Care
description: HACLA program linking rental assistance with supportive services for chronically homeless persons with disabilities
benefit_formula: Estimated annual housing assistance plus supportive services value
